intro: Mulinum gandogeri, also known as Mulinum, is a plant species belonging to the family Apiaceae, which is commonly found in the Andes mountains of South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Mulinum gandogeri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Mulinum gandogeri include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions.
